steps.title: Determine slope of line k from the image
steps.content: Identify two points on line k from the image to calculate its slope $ m_k $. For perpendicular lines, the product of their slopes satisfies $ m_k \cdot m_j = -1 $. If $ m_k = -\frac{3}{4} $, then $ m_j = \frac{4}{3} $, which matches choices A and B.

steps.title: Use point-slope form for line j
steps.content: Plug in the point $(-15, -28)$ and slope $ \frac{4}{3} $ into $ y = \frac{4}{3}x + b $: $$ -28 = \frac{4}{3}(-15) + b $$ $$ -28 = -20 + b $$ $$ b = -8 $$. The equation is $ y = \frac{4}{3}x - 8 $.

steps.title: Match to the correct choice
steps.content: The derived equation $ y = \frac{4}{3}x - 8 $ matches choice A exactly.
